pattern_recognition: Trie Data Structure; it efficiently handles prefix searches and supports dynamic word addition, which is essential for the add and search operations with wildcard support.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Check each word in the list for a match, replacing '.' with any character.
approaches.algorithm: Iterate through the list of words, for each word, check if it matches the search string by replacing '.' with all possible characters.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Use a Trie to store words, allowing for efficient prefix searching and wildcard handling.
approaches.algorithm: Insert each word into the Trie. For search, traverse the Trie recursively, treating '.' as a wildcard that can match any character.

dry_run: Consider the input operations: addWord('bad'), addWord('dad'), addWord('mad'), search('pad'). The search will traverse the Trie, but since 'pad' is not in the Trie, it will return false. For search('bad'), it will find the word and return true.
edge_cases: Empty input should return false for search. A single element should correctly return true if it matches. Duplicates should not affect the structure but should be handled in search. Large inputs should be efficiently managed by the Trie structure. Overflow is not a concern here as we are dealing with strings.
common_pitfalls: 1) Not handling the '.' wildcard correctly, leading to incorrect search results. 2) Failing to account for case sensitivity in word addition and search. 3) Inefficiently searching through the list instead of using the Trie structure, leading to poor performance.
